content:
# Transaction Sharing Story
## Description
As a personal finance app user, I want to share my transactions with other users, so that I can manage my finances collaboratively.

## Acceptance Criteria

### AC1: Share Single Transaction
- **Given** I am logged in and have at least one transaction
- **When** I select a transaction to share and enter a recipient's username or email
- **Then** The selected transaction is shared with the recipient
- **And** I receive a confirmation of the shared transaction

### AC2: Share Multiple Transactions
- **Given** I am logged in and have multiple transactions
- **When** I select multiple transactions to share and enter a recipient's username or email
- **Then** All selected transactions are shared with the recipient
- **And** I receive a confirmation of the shared transactions

### AC3: Access Control for Shared Transactions
- **Given** I have shared transactions with another user
- **When** The recipient attempts to access the shared transactions
- **Then** The system checks the recipient's access level before granting access to the shared transactions
- **And** The recipient can only view the shared transactions if they have the required access level

### AC4: Error Handling for Invalid Recipient
- **Given** I attempt to share a transaction with an invalid recipient
- **When** The system checks the recipient's username or email
- **Then** The system displays an error message indicating that the recipient is invalid
- **And** The transaction is not shared

### AC5: Edge Case - Sharing Transactions with Different Permissions
- **Given** I have multiple accounts with different permissions (read-only, read-write)
- **When** I share transactions from these accounts with another user
- **Then** The system applies the correct permissions to each shared transaction
- **And** The recipient can only access the shared transactions according to the assigned permissions

## Relevant docs
Feature spec: [Personal Finance App Specification](https://example.com/spec)

---

# Multi-Account Sharing Story
## Description
As a personal finance app user, I want to share multiple accounts with different permissions, so that I can manage my finances collaboratively with fine-grained control.

## Acceptance Criteria

### AC1: Share Multiple Accounts with Read-Only Permission
- **Given** I have multiple accounts with read-only permission
- **When** I select these accounts to share with another user
- **Then** The selected accounts are shared with the recipient with read-only permission
- **And** The recipient can only view the shared accounts

### AC2: Share Multiple Accounts with Read-Write Permission
- **Given** I have multiple accounts with read-write permission
- **When** I select these accounts to share with another user
- **Then** The selected accounts are shared with the recipient with read-write permission
- **And** The recipient can view and edit the shared accounts

### AC3: Access Control for Shared Accounts
- **Given** I have shared multiple accounts with another user
- **When** The recipient attempts to access the shared accounts
- **Then** The system checks the recipient's access level before granting access to the shared accounts
- **And** The recipient can only access the shared accounts according to the assigned permissions

### AC4: Error Handling for Invalid Account Selection
- **Given** I attempt to share an invalid account
- **When** The system checks the account selection
- **Then** The system displays an error message indicating that the account is invalid
- **And** The account is not shared

### AC5: Edge Case - Sharing Accounts with Mixed Permissions
- **Given** I have multiple accounts with mixed permissions (read-only, read-write)
- **When** I share these accounts with another user
- **Then** The system applies the correct permissions to each shared account
- **And** The recipient can only access the shared accounts according to the assigned permissions

## Relevant docs
Feature spec: [Personal Finance App Specification](https://example.com/spec)

---

# Access Control Story
## Description
As a personal finance app user, I want the system to control access to my shared transactions and accounts, so that I can ensure the security and privacy of my financial data.

## Acceptance Criteria

### AC1: Access Level Check for Shared Transactions
- **Given** I have shared transactions with another user
- **When** The recipient attempts to access the shared transactions
- **Then** The system checks the recipient's access level before granting access to the shared transactions
- **And** The recipient can only view the shared transactions if they have the required access level

### AC2: Access Level Check for Shared Accounts
- **Given** I have shared accounts with another user
- **When** The recipient attempts to access the shared accounts
- **Then** The system checks the recipient's access level before granting access to the shared accounts
- **And** The recipient can only access the shared accounts according to the assigned permissions

### AC3: Error Handling for Insufficient Access Level
- **Given** The recipient attempts to access shared transactions or accounts without the required access level
- **When** The system checks the recipient's access level
- **Then** The system displays an error message indicating that the recipient lacks the required access level
- **And** The recipient is denied access to the shared transactions or accounts

### AC4: Edge Case - Access Level Change for Shared Transactions and Accounts
- **Given** I have shared transactions and accounts with another user
- **When** I change the recipient's access level
- **Then** The system updates the access level for the shared transactions and accounts
- **And** The recipient's access to the shared transactions and accounts is adjusted accordingly

## Relevant docs
Feature spec: [Personal Finance App Specification](https://example.com/spec)
